channel.title: dotnet/runtime Pull Requests
channel.description: Pull Requests from https://github.com/dotnet/runtime, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: PR: Improve MetricEventSource error handling - @noahfalk
item.description: Enabling multiple instances of a collection tool was causing both tools to lose their connections and it wasn't obvious from within the tool what had happened. I modified the behavior so that once a tool connects successfully it should never be disconnected and made a distinctive error event so that the additional tool instances can easily identify they are trying an unsupported operation.

item.title: PR: fix queue converter singleton initialization - @eiriktsarpalis
PR: Fix DeserializeAsyncEnumerable streaming behavior - @eiriktsarpalis
item.description:
Fixes regression introduced by #51702: the ContinueDeserialize method will return 'null' on partial reads so no elements will be yielded by the enumerator until the entire stream has been consumed.

This change reverts to the original implementation where the partially populated queue is being fetched from ReadStack instead.

Fixes #56055.

item.title: PR: Disable [QUIC] WriteCanceled_NextWriteThrows on Mock - @karelz
item.description: Disabled test tracked by #55995

item.title: PR: [release/6.0-preview7] [QUIC] Remove AppContext switch from S.N.Quic - @github-actions[bot]
item.description:
Backport of #56027 to release/6.0-preview7

/cc @ManickaP

Customer Impact

Short version: ASP.NET cannot deterministically enable System.Net.Quic for their HTTP/3 scenarios, leading to exceptions.

Long version: System.Net.Quic will be internal-only in .NET 6.0 (as implementation detail of client and server HTTP/3). We want to prevent customers from accidentally using it. We have implemented 2 levels of protection for System.Net.Quic to make it INTERNAL -- we removed ref assemblies from public and we used AppContext switch which is off by default (also used for HTTP/3 in HttpClient). ASP.NET uses different mechanism for opt-in for HTTP/3 in .NET 6.0. Their mechanism cannot safely and deterministically turn on the System.Net.Quic AppContext as it relies on order of type initializers. Therefore we agreed to remove the AppContext protection for System.Net.Quic (it is in place for HttpClient's HTTP/3 still) and leave only one level of protection for System.Net.Quic being internal (removed ref assemblies).

Testing

CI

Risk

Low

item.title: PR: [iOS/tvOS/MacCatalyst] Disable some of the library tests due to failures - @MaximLipnin
item.description:
It's to clean up the respective CI staging lanes.

The related issues:

  • https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/56073
  • https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/56048
  • https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/55712
  • https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/56084
  • https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/56085

item.title: PR: [release/6.0-preview7] [debugger] Export mono_debugger_agent_unhandled_exception to avoid usage of Debugger.Mono_UnhandledException - @thaystg
item.description:
Backport of #55963 to release/6.0-preview7

/cc @thaystg

Customer Impact

Xamarin android will be able to call this function and avoid calling Debugger.Mono_UnhandledException as this does not exist on net6.

Testing

Risk

Zero risk, it's just exporting and existing function.

item.title: PR: Add back regex tests from reverted .* optimization - @stephentoub
item.description: Putting back @pgovind's new tests from the reverted #51508

item.title: PR: Fix outerloop System.Buffers tests after s_trimBuffers removal - @stephentoub
item.description:
We deleted this switch. Turns out there were outerloop tests looking for it.

cc: @jkotas

item.title: PR: Re-enable tests for runtime handles in crossgen2 - @davidwrighton
item.description:
These tests pass now. This issue was fixed some time ago.

Fixes #43460

item.title: PR: disable SendMoreThanStreamLimitRequests_Succeeds on Mock - @wfurt
item.description:
Disable on Mock. There was only on MsQuic failure and that can be general instability of QUIC.

fixes #55957

item.title: PR: [wasm] Few misc build improvements - @radical
item.description: .. including Add back --emit-llvm that got removed mistakenly, in an earlier commit.

item.title: PR: Fix an issue where we are not injecting more HTTP2 connections when we should - @geoffkizer
item.description:
Fixes #55980

This is a regression caused by my connection pooling changes.

For HTTP2, we constrain our connection injection logic to never inject more than one connection at a time. On successful connection establishment, we should check if we need to create yet another connection to handle the queued requests. Unfortunately, we aren't doing this today.

The specific problem is a scenario like this:

(1) User submits one request. We enqueue it and kick off an HTTP2 connection attempt. (2) User submits 100 more requests. There are now 101 requests in the queue. (3) Connection attempt succeeds. We dequeue 100 requests and give them to the new connection to be processed. (4) There's still one request in the queue, so we should be kicking off another connection attempt -- but currently we don't.

item.title: PR: Convert the last crossgen tests to use crossgen2 - @davidwrighton
item.description:
• take advantage of the AlwaysUseCrossGen2 flag instead of manually updating all the crossgen commands
  • remove version bubbles test. We have much more complex testing for that scenario now
  • remove regular crossgen logic from the script generator

Fixes #743

item.title: PR: add timeout handling to QuicStream - @wfurt
item.description: I know this is low priority but I did it as experiment while working on #55642 to at least prevent infinite hang. I did not want to loose so we may turn it into deaf and wait. The scope and risk is small IMHO as it mimics what NetworkStream does. The behavior is covered by conformance tests that would light up on CanTimeout

item.title: PR: Update CG2 ArgIterator to match the runtime one - @janvorli
item.description: The goal of the change is primarily to make it correct for the Apple Silicon where the calling convention slightly differs from the Unix ARM64 one, especially in passing arguments smaller than a pointer size on stack.

item.title: PR: Consume PosixSignal in Hosting's ConsoleLifetime - @eerhardt
item.description:
This fixes quite a few Hosting exit issues related to ConsoleLifetime.

It is probably best to review the first commit by itself - Add NetCoreAppCurrent target to Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting. I needed to add a net6.0 target so I could use the new Signal APIs from #50527.

The rest of the commits fix the individual bugs.

Fix #55417 Fix #44086 Fix #50397 Fix #42224 Fix #35990

cc @stephentoub @jkotas - FYI in case you want to take a look

item.title: PR: [WIP] Make sure ServerGCHeapDetails is up to date - @cshung
item.description:
Fixes #54368

@dotnet/dotnet-diag

item.title: PR: Fix EH issues with SPMI and crossgen2 - @davidwrighton
item.description:
There are two major adjustments here

  1. Disable catching SPMI exceptions in the JIT, and instead catch them in SPMI, using a new jit interface api
  2. Generalize the change @BruceForstall made in #49531 to apply to all situations where SPMI attempts to filter exceptions.

With these changes catching exceptions should be more reliable. However, I was unable to reproduce the failure, so I need some jit team expertise to identify if these fixes are good.#56050

@BruceForstall does this solve your concerns? Could you review/test the changes to make sure they are behaving as expected?

item.title: PR: Revert Version.TryFormat changes - @stephentoub
item.description:
Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/56011

I'd updated the implementation to use the new span-based interpolated string handler. The code was simpler/cleaner, but the code was already fairly-tuned direct span-based code, and while the extra overhead was a few nanoseconds, that was a measurable percentage of the overall time. So, I'm reverting it so as not to regress. And while here, I made a few tweaks that give it a small extra boost.

item.title: PR: crossgen2: Don't set onlyParseItemsDefinedInAssembly in Composite mode - @EgorBo
item.description:
Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/55695

I have a repro: https://github.com/EgorBo/StaticPGO_Example - it consists of a console app and a lib. When I run crossgen2 in Composite mode on it - PGO data is just ignored.

Here is the command executed by msbuild during "dotnet publish":

_CreateR2RImages:
  C:\Users\bogat\.nuget\packages\microsoft.netcore.app.crossgen2.win-x64\6.0.0-rc.1.21369.2\tools\crossgen2.exe --targetos:windows
  --targetarch:x64
  -O
  --embed-pgo-data
  --mibc:C:\prj\StaticPGO_Example\App\my.mibc
  --composite
  --out:"obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\R2R\App.r2r.dll"
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\Lib.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\App.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.Collections.Immutable.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.Console.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.Diagnostics.StackTrace.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.IO.Compression.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.IO.MemoryMappedFiles.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.Private.CoreLib.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.Reflection.Metadata.dll
  obj\Release\net6.0\win-x64\linked\System.Runtime.dll

Lib is the first assembly in the list of input assemblies here and it's set as onlyParseItemsDefinedInAssembly:

My fix sets onlyParseItemsDefinedInAssembly to null in Composite mode (--compilebubblegenerics also does it).

As the result, here is the new R2R asm code for Test: https://gist.github.com/EgorBo/6b380e47a3be83720921ea6bb2c76678 - PGO data is used, calls are devirtualized.

Here is the previous (Main) codegen: https://gist.github.com/EgorBo/1d66953706e093a6e5cd284e2ba41105

/cc @AndyAyersMS

item.title: PR: check handle before shutdown in quic connection Dispose - @wfurt
item.description:
fixes observed NRE failure in CI https://helixre8s23ayyeko0k025g8.blob.core.windows.net/dotnet-runtime-refs-pull-55877-merge-4b55bf7e17664f8d97/System.Net.Http.Functional.Tests/console.44cb6dd0.log?sv=2019-07-07&se=2021-08-09T20%3A59%3A05Z&sr=c&sp=rl&sig=fcVOgCzStvmdaDVp%2FQRDS0Df0FtzBFqHVkrAh4pCysk%3D

      Condition(s) not met: "CanTestCertificates"
Unhandled exception. System.ArgumentNullException: SafeHandle cannot be null. (Parameter 'pHandle')
   at System.StubHelpers.StubHelpers.SafeHandleAddRef(SafeHandle pHandle, Boolean& success) in /_/src/coreclr/System.Private.CoreLib/src/System/StubHelpers.cs:line 1273
   at System.Net.Quic.Implementations.MsQuic.MsQuicConnection.Dispose(Boolean disposing) in /_/src/libraries/System.Net.Quic/src/System/Net/Quic/Implementations/MsQuic/MsQuicConnection.cs:line 715
   at System.Net.Quic.Implementations.MsQuic.MsQuicConnection.Finalize() in /_/src/libraries/System.Net.Quic/src/System/Net/Quic/Implementations/MsQuic/MsQuicConnection.cs:line 684
./RunTests.sh: line 162:    22 Aborted                 (core dumped) "$RUNTIME_PATH/dotnet" exec --runtimeconfig System.Net.Http.Functional.Tests.runtimeconfig.json --depsfile System.Net.Http.Functional.Tests.deps.json xunit.console.dll System.Net.Http.Functional.Tests.dll -xml testResults.xml -nologo -nocolor -notrait category=IgnoreForCI -notrait category=OuterLoop -notrait category=failing $RSP_FILE
/root/helix/work/workitem
----- end Tue Jul 20 21:05:32 UTC 2021 ----- exit code 134 ----------------------------------------------------------
exit code 134 means SIGABRT Abort. Managed or native assert, or runtime check such as heap corruption, caused call to abort(). Core dumped.

When constructor for outbound connection fail, _state.Handle can be null and this would be called from finalizer.

item.title: PR: Disable implicit namespace imports - @safern
item.description:
In preview7 SDK some namespace imports are defined by default. Most of our projects don't use implicit references as we are low in the stack and producing those references. This can cause issues when building with a preview7 SDK, i.e:

D:\repos\dotnet_runtime_3\artifacts\obj\System.IO.Pipelines\ref\net6.0-Debug\System.IO.Pipelines.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(5,29): error CS0234: The type or namespace name 'Linq' does not exist in the namespace 'System' (are you missing an assembly reference?) [D:\repos\dotnet_runtime_3\src\libraries\System.IO.Pipelines\ref\System.IO.Pipelines.csproj]

This was introduced to the SDK last week. https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/commit/85943958e5a01d6bbd39a947aec2a4a37b8ba7cf

cc: @dotnet/runtime-infrastructure

item.title: PR: Import local nodes that may need normalization through common path - @jakobbotsch
item.description:
Fix issues where we were creating TYP_INT locals that need normalization in the importer. This could lead to us to read the wrong number of bytes from such locals.

In particular this happened when passing a small-sized parameter as a TYP_INT argument, and that call was inlined. We would end up with a TYP_INT GT_LCL_VAR node referring to the small parameter that required normalization.

This change seems to have some benefit too; in a few situations, it seems the old behavior meant that we could not enregister the small locals.

Fix #55143

item.title: PR: quic test improvements - @wfurt
item.description:
This is test-only change

  • Fix pattern when we can hang in AcceptAsync if client fails to connect
  • plumb more ITestOutputHelper so we can capture more data on failures
  • add some more timeouts in places
  • restrict wildcard on Conformance tests so editor can create swap file without breaking build.

contributes to #55642

item.title: PR: Disable Http2_ServerSendsInvalidSettingsValue_Error - @karelz
item.description: Tracked by #1581

item.title: PR: [release/6.0-preview7] Update analyzer rulesets - @github-actions[bot]
item.description:
Backport of #55925 to release/6.0-preview7

/cc @pgovind @stephentoub

Customer Impact

The latest analyzer bits will cause dotnet/runtime's build to fail with warnings. With https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/pull/19084, the latest analyzer bits will make it to the sdk's P7 branch. I'm not sure if an update to the sdk's branch will trigger a dotnet/runtime build, but I want to be safe here anyway and turn off the DetectPreviewFeatures analyzer for dotnet/runtime. We'll turn on preview features and iterate on the analyzer over the RC1 timeframe to fix the warnings for RC1

Testing

No product change here, not sure there's anything to test

Risk

Minimal. We're already doing this in upstream/main.

item.title: PR: [release/6.0-preview7] Fix System.Data.SqlClient dependency version on compat pack - @github-actions[bot]
item.description:
Backport of #55956 to release/6.0-preview7

/cc @safern

Customer Impact

They can't reference the package as it fails with:

Microsoft.Windows.Compatibility 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.12 nuget package depends on System.Data.SqlClient 4.8.2-preview.6.21352.12 which is not available on nuget.org.

Testing

Manually tested that the dependency is correct now and we also enabled package testing on CI to ensure the package can be restored correctly.

Risk

Low.

item.title: PR: Revert "Eliminate backtracking in the interpreter for patterns with .… - @pgovind
item.description:
…* (#51508)"

This reverts commit 7eb749c8b78609865edcad67f57084aa382632a3.

cc @BrennanConroy @danmoseley @jeffhandley @tannergooding

I'll add the regression as a unit test in a separate PR and investigate a fix locally (will probably only target .NET7P1 at this point for the optimization changes)

item.title: PR: Revert "Eliminate backtracking in the interpreter for patterns with .* " - @stephentoub
item.description:
Reverts dotnet/runtime#51508

Edit: cc @BrennanConroy

item.title: PR: Revert "[release/6.0-preview7] Eliminate backtracking in the interpreter for patterns with .* " - @stephentoub
item.description: Reverts dotnet/runtime#55960

item.title: PR: [QUIC] Remove AppContext switch from S.N.Quic - @ManickaP
item.description:
AppContext switch for H/3 scoped down to S.N.Http only. S.N.Quic doesn't have any extra guards ATM.

@karelz Could you please help with servicing?

cc: @davidfowl

item.title: PR: Upgrade to msbuild version 16.10 to fix big-endian bug - @uweigand
item.description:
• Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/54826

  • Adjust net472 dependencies of Microsoft.NETCore.Platforms.Tests

item.title: PR: Remove diagnostic printfs inadvertently left in PGO code. - @AndyAyersMS
PR: Check test failures by disabling all - @agocke
item.description: No review necessary, I'm trying to evaluate the tests results

item.title: PR: [release/6.0-preview7] [wasm] Add support for using custom native libraries - @radical
PR: Start codesign from executable on apphost creation - @mateoatr
item.description:
UseShellExecute should be false whenever we are redirecting to standard error (as we are doing when firing codesign when creating an apphost).

Fixes #55992.

item.title: PR: Hostfxr package should depend on host package - @NikolaMilosavljevic
item.description:
Hostfxr RPM package should have a dependency on the host package.

This is a fix for a regression in 6.0 packaging infra.

Old 5.0 infra had this dependency specified in the corresponding json file: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/74905e767eb7b971636310965b66bf5dfd6a3545/src/installer/pkg/packaging/rpm/dotnet-hostfxr-rpm_config.json#L34-L37

item.title: PR: Re-enable async method state machine clearing test - @stephentoub
item.description: Closes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/30122

item.title: PR: Disable test Connect_DualMode_DnsConnect_RetrievedEndPoints_Success on Linux - @karelz
item.description: Disabled test tracked by #54677 and #55709

item.title: PR: Disable test Connect_DualMode_MultiAddressFamilyConnect_RetrievedEndPoints_Success on Linux - @karelz
item.description: Disabled test tracked by #55053

item.title: PR: Disable SendStreamLimitRequestsConcurrently_Succeeds - @karelz
item.description: Disabled test tracked by #56000

item.title: PR: Disable QuicStreamTests_MsQuicProvider.ReadOutstanding_ReadAborted_Throws - @karelz
item.description:
Disable test: System.Net.Quic.Tests.QuicStreamTests_MsQuicProvider.ReadOutstanding_ReadAborted_Throws

Tracked by #55948

item.title: PR: [QUIC] Fix cancelling shutdown completion source - @CarnaViire
PR: Eliminate code duplication of OnWriteResume methods of collection converters - @eiriktsarpalis
item.description:
Most of our collection converters replicate line-by-line the IEnumerator-based implementation of the OnWriteResume methods. This PR makes minor refactorings to accommodate the default implementations in the converter base classes.

Should contribute towards mitigating #55937 cc @eerhardt

item.title: PR: Disallow widening for explicit tailcalls - @jakobbotsch
item.description:
It is a runtime detail that the managed calling convention widens return values, so only allow this behavior for opportunistic tailcalls.

Fix #55253

item.title: PR: Fix tizen arm32 issue. - @sandreenko
item.description:
Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/55794, introduced by https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/55558.

I have decided to go with this fix (see discussion in https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/55794#issuecomment-882856171) for now because:

  1. RC1 and the other fixes are more disturbing;
  2. fix during morph:fgInitArgInfo looks like:
ASG(LCL_VAR double temp, originalArg)
LCL_FLD long temp
CALL

and it causes the argument to be read from memory when before it could stay in float registers. 3. it is dangerous to do morph arg changes when we don't have actual armel testing.

item.title: PR: mitigation for quic tests hangs - @wfurt
item.description:
It seems like the hang is when we run synchronous conformance tests. We receive notification, we reset the completion source but the WriteAsync task would never wake up and be finished.

> threadpool -wi
logStart: 191
logSize: 200
CPU utilization: 0 %
Worker Thread: Total: 2 Running: 2 Idle: 0 MaxLimit: 32767 MinLimit: 2
Work Request in Queue: 0

Queued work items:
Queue Address Work Item
[Global] 00007f4468a32b20 System.Runtime.CompilerServices.AsyncTaskMethodBuilder`1+AsyncStateMachineBox`1[[System.Threading.Tasks.VoidTaskResult, System.Private.CoreLib],[System.Net.Quic.Implementations.MsQuic.MsQuicStream+<WriteAsync>d__21, System.Net.Quic]]
[Global] 00007f4468a352e8 System.Runtime.CompilerServices.AsyncTaskMethodBuilder`1+AsyncStateMachineBox`1[[System.Threading.Tasks.VoidTaskResult, System.Private.CoreLib],[System.Net.Quic.Implementations.MsQuic.MsQuicStream+<WriteAsync>d__21, System.Net.Quic]]
[Global] 00007f44688ab968 System.Runtime.CompilerServices.AsyncTaskMethodBuilder`1+AsyncStateMachineBox`1[[System.Threading.Tasks.VoidTaskResult, System.Private.CoreLib],[System.Net.Quic.Tests.QuicStreamTests`1+<>c__DisplayClass14_0+<<ReadWrite_Random_Success>b__1>d[[System.Net.Quic.Tests.MsQuicProviderFactory, System.Net.Quic.Functional.Tests]], System.Net.Quic.Functional.Tests]]
[Global] 00007f44689e8d68 System.Threading.QueueUserWorkItemCallbackDefaultContext`1[[System.Object, System.Private.CoreLib]] => System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1+ValueTaskSourceAsTask+<>c[[System.Int32, System.Private.CoreLib]].<.cctor>b__4_0(System.Object)
Statistics:
MT Count TotalSize Class Name
00007f4497eb4a00 1 32 System.Threading.QueueUserWorkItemCallbackDefaultContext`1[[System.Object, System.Private.CoreLib]]
00007f4497f4c580 1 96 System.Runtime.CompilerServices.AsyncTaskMethodBuilder`1+AsyncStateMachineBox`1[[System.Threading.Tasks.VoidTaskResult, System.Private.CoreLib],[System.Net.Quic.Tests.QuicStreamTests`1+<>c__DisplayClass14_0+<<ReadWrite_Random_Success>b__1>d[[System.Net.Quic.Tests.MsQuicProviderFactory, System.Net.Quic.Functional.Tests]], System.Net.Quic.Functional.Tests]]
00007f4497b02620 2 192 System.Runtime.CompilerServices.AsyncTaskMethodBuilder`1+AsyncStateMachineBox`1[[System.Threading.Tasks.VoidTaskResult, System.Private.CoreLib],[System.Net.Quic.Implementations.MsQuic.MsQuicStream+<WriteAsync>d__21, System.Net.Quic]]
Total 4 objects

big thanks to @stephentoub who pointed to #53471 and suggested this mitigation. This is not final fix but I did few hundreds runs without problem when it would generally hang in less then 10 before on my dev box. This should at least improve PR experience and CI failure rate. I will follow-up with details to help find better fix e.g. thread pool.

contributes to #55642

item.title: PR: [WIP] test if macos NRE happens due to R2R - @mangod9
item.description: Disabling R2R when running crossgen2. Not intended to merge

item.title: PR: reenable ConnectTimeout_PlaintextStreamFilterTimesOut_Throws test - @geoffkizer
item.description:
I believe the flakiness is caused because in very slow environments (like certain CI machines), the cancellation token may fire before the server accepts the connection, which will cause the server logic to hang.

Fix this by using ConnectCallback to avoid creating a real connection at all.

Fixes #55931

item.title: PR: Remove QuicStream.ShutdownWriteCompleted method - @geoffkizer
